# Predictions Scorecard

*Generated by Claude Code from the Ask Sam archive using agent teams — a multi-agent deep dive across 793 episodes.*

---

## Whiffed It

### Snapchat (2013)

> "My username still has the word 'test' in it because I was so certain it was stupid. Just for kids and dick pics."
>
> — Sam Parr

Snapchat reached 750M+ monthly active users and a $20B+ market cap. Sam's username is still `samtest`.

### Bitcoin (2012–2013)

> "Guys in my office were telling me about it in 2012, 2013, and I was kind of laughing."
>
> — Sam Parr

Bitcoin was ~$13 in early 2013. It crossed $100,000 in 2024.

### SBF & FTX (2021 Milly Awards)

> "He's probably one of the two most interesting people in the world."
>
> — Sam Parr & Shaan Puri, 2021 Milly Awards

Sam Bankman-Fried was sentenced to 25 years in federal prison for fraud in March 2024. The Milly Award did not age well.

### Beehiiv (2022)

> "I predicted there wasn't a market. So far it seems like I was."
>
> — Sam Parr (later reversed himself)

Beehiiv hit $25M+ ARR and raised at a $200M+ valuation. Sam publicly walked back the prediction.

### Angel Investing Track Record (2016–2023)

> Invested roughly $1M across angel deals since 2016 with minimal returns. Missed Whatnot (now ~$10B valuation — would have been 200x+). Missed Shuffle (crypto casino doing massive daily volume). Called his own track record "terrible."
>
> — 2023 Milly Awards recap

### Castro Podcast App

> Sunk ~$2M into the Castro podcast player over 5 years. Called it a "money bonfire."
>
> — 2023 Milly Awards recap

---

## Called It

### Epic Gardening (2024)

> "In about five years he's going to be a really big deal — like Chip and Joanna."
>
> — Sam Parr on Kevin Espiritu

Epic Gardening hit ~$45M ARR and became the largest gardening media brand in the US.

### The Hustle → HubSpot (2021)

Built The Hustle to 1.5M+ subscribers, sold to HubSpot for a reported $27M. Proved the newsletter-to-exit model before it was a category.

### Boring Businesses (2020–present)

Sam and Shaan were early and loud on "boring businesses" — laundromats, HVAC, storage, pest control — before the trend went mainstream on Twitter/X. Codie Sanchez and Nick Huber became recurring guests building on this thesis.

### Hampton (2023–present)

> Launched Hampton as a peer community for founders doing $1M–$100M+. Bootstrapped to profitability. Proved his own thesis that community businesses are underrated.

---

## How This Was Generated

This scorecard was produced by Claude Code with the [Ask Sam](https://github.com/cdeistopened/ask-sam) plugin repo. The query was: *"What predictions did Sam and Shaan get wrong? What did they get right?"*

Claude Code spawned an agent team that cross-referenced episodes across the full 793-episode archive, pulled direct quotes with source attribution, and scored each prediction against known outcomes.

The repo contains 18 interactive skills, 134 framework articles, and 38 people profiles — all queryable. Clone it and ask your own questions.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/cdeistopened/ask-sam.git
```
